title: Transformers: Rescue Bots Academy
synopsis: A new generation of young Autobot recruits—Hot Shot, Whirl, Hoist, Medix, and Wedge—enroll in the Rescue Bot Training Academy on Griffin Rock, where they are mentored by the veteran Rescue Bots. Through teamwork, trial and error, and plenty of misadventures, the recruits learn that being a hero means more than just strength—it takes heart, courage, and cooperation.

episodes.title: Recruits, Part 1
episodes.synopsis: Five new Autobot recruits arrive at the Rescue Bot Training Academy to begin their training.

episodes.title: Recruits, Part 2

episodes.title: Tough Luck Chuck

facts: Rescue Bots Academy premiered with a special preview on December 8, 2018, before regular episodes began January 5, 2019.
The series was animated by Boulder Media Studio using 2D animation, with episodes running 11 minutes each (paired into 22-minute half-hour blocks).
Unlike the original Rescue Bots, the voice cast was drawn from a non-union New York casting pool, matching the approach used for the concurrent series Cyberverse.
The series serves as a direct sequel to Transformers: Rescue Bots, with returning characters Heatwave, Chase, Boulder, Blades, and Cody Burns in new mentoring roles.
Episode order differs significantly depending on the platform—US broadcast, UK broadcast, Netflix, iTunes, and YouTube each used different orderings.
Rescue Bots Academy continued the tradition of Transformers programming aimed at preschool-age viewers, bridging the gap between Aligned and Modern era series.
The series was developed by Nicole Dubuc, who co-created the original Rescue Bots series.
